commit.message:
feat: Multi-agent quant framework — Markov regimes, crypto factors, Almgren-Chriss impact, stablecoin hedging, orchestrator (#4)

* feat(research): add Markov-Switching regime detection module

Implements a Bayesian HMM with four market states (LOW_VOL_HIGH_LIQ,
MID_VOL_MID_LIQ, HIGH_VOL_LOW_LIQ, CRISIS) using vol-ratio and Amihud
illiquidity as observable signals.

Key components:
- MarkovRegimeState enum with 4 crypto-calibrated states
- AmihudIlliquidity class (Amihud 2002)
- MarkovRegimeDetector with Bayesian forward-step updates
- Pre-calibrated 4x4 transition matrix for crypto markets
- Gaussian emission model per state
- Confidence-weighted portfolio aggregation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add crypto-native alpha factors module

Implement 10 crypto-specific alpha factors in crypto_factors.py:
- NvtRatioFactor: NVT proxy using price/volume
- OrderImbalanceFactor: Volume-weighted buy/sell pressure
- FundingRateSpreadFactor: Two-tiered funding rate spread
- AmihudIlliquidityFactor: Amihud (2002) illiquidity ratio
- VolatilityOfVolatilityFactor: Vol-of-vol instability signal
- RsiFactory: RSI centered around zero
- BollingerBandWidthFactor: Bollinger Band compression
- VwapDeviationFactor: Price deviation from VWAP
- OBVTrendFactor: On-Balance Volume trend slope
- CTrendAggregateFactor: Elastic Net regularised multi-factor aggregate

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Almgren-Chriss non-linear market impact model and enhanced execution simulator

Implements:
- MarketImpactConfig: calibration parameters (gamma, eta, exponent, fees, volume cap)
- compute_market_impact: square-root impact law with volume participation cap
- EnhancedExecutionSimulator: rebalance with non-linear slippage and funding costs
- EnhancedSimulatedFill: fill with full cost attribution
- compute_adv: average daily volume from OHLCV bars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add dynamic stablecoin hedging and volatility scaling module

Implement VolatilityScaler, StablecoinHedger, and AdaptivePortfolioConstructor
for regime-aware crypto portfolio risk management.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add multi-agent orchestration framework for iterative strategy development

Implements TaskStatus enum, TaskNode DAG dataclass, MemoryAgent for
cross-iteration learning, AuditAgent for performance diagnosis,
DAGPlanner for building and managing task DAGs, IterationResult
snapshot, and Orchestrator coordinator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add parameter perturbation robustness validation module

Implements Monte Carlo parameter sensitivity analysis:
- PerturbationConfig/PerturbationResult frozen dataclasses
- perturb_parameters(): randomly jitters params by ±pct
- run_perturbation_analysis(): runs N backtests with perturbed params,
  computes distribution stats, checks degradation thresholds
- format_perturbation_report(): human-readable summary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive tests for new research modules

Create 6 test files covering:
- test_markov_regime.py: Markov-Switching HMM regime detection (17 tests)
- test_crypto_factors.py: All 10 crypto-native alpha factors (30 tests)
- test_market_impact.py: Almgren-Chriss market impact model (20 tests)
- test_stablecoin_hedge.py: Vol scaling and stablecoin hedging (18 tests)
- test_orchestrator.py: Multi-agent orchestration framework (27 tests)
- test_robustness.py: Parameter perturbation analysis (11 tests)

All tests use inline GBM synthetic data, class-based grouping,
and pytest style following existing conventions.

commit.message:
fix(web): dialog stays visible after closing due to Tailwind flex override

Tailwind's `flex` class overrides the browser's native
`dialog:not([open]) { display: none }` rule, causing the dialog
to remain visible after dialog.close() is called.

Replace `flex` with `hidden open:flex` so the dialog is hidden
when closed and uses flexbox layout only when open.

commit.message:
fix(sidebar): use counts and minimal-list APIs DEV-1772 (#6918)

### 📣 Summary

Use new API for Sidebar lists of projects (Deployed, Draft, and
Archived). This fixes a problem when user with more than 200 projects
was unable to see all of their projects in the Sidebar (previously it
was limited to 200 latest projects, prioritizing Deployed ones). Now
three groups use infinite scroll loading. We also fixed a problem with
sidebar serving always "My Projects" projects - now when user switches
to organization projects or a custom view, the sidebar reflects this
properly.

### 💭 Notes

Changes here:
- `AssetName` and `getAssetDisplayName` now works with
`AssetMinimalList`
- `BulkDeletePrompt` invalidates all sidebar related queries now
- `SidebarFormsList` has multiple changes:
- It now operates on context (`my-projects`, `my-org-projects`, or
`custom-view-projects`) which is resolved from current path
  - Has a utility function for invalidating all queries in sidebar
- Has a custom `useQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that operates
on different function based on context
  - Reflux actions are now invalidating with utility function
- It now only fetches counts from new APIs (different counts API for
different context) and passes those to `SidebarFormsListCategory`
- `SidebarFormsListCategory` has multiple changes too:
  - It operates now on context and deployment status
- Instead of getting list of assets from parent, it fetches it now using
API
- Has a custom `useInfiniteQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that
operates on different function based on context and is triggered by
`InfiniteScrollTrigger`
- The list of projects now always lead to `ROUTES.FORM`, as minimal list
endpoint doesn't serve permissions

### 👀 Preview steps

1. ℹ️ Have an account with a lot of projects, ideally over 200 (use
kobo-fixture, or drag and drop a lot of small projects [many
small.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26484188/many.small.zip)
or some other idea)
2. [Enable
MMO](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Setting-up-MMOs-for-local-development-1357e515f6548093b631f1686b60e49d)
3. Create new user, add it to MMO, create few projects with the new
user, go back to original user afterwards
4. [Enable a custom
view](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Regional-Projects-User-Documentation-Setting-up-Custom-Project-Views-f45b8a2bd09d45a4ade89f4b4430df1d)
with only part of projects being included (see "South Asia" example in
the link)
5. Change few projects to have the "Country" field matching the custom
view
6. Go to My Projects
7. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/assets/?q=asset_type:survey&limit=200` is no longer being called
8. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/counts/` is being called
9. Open "Deployed" category
10. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/minimal-list/` is being
called and a list of projects appear
11. Scroll down
12. 🟢 Notice that another API call is being made and next 20 projects
are loaded (infinite scroll)
13. Select one project in the table (with checkbox)
14. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
15. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
16. Select two projects in the table (also with checkbox; bulk deleting
uses different code)
17. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
18. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
19. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "<username>'s organization
projects"
20. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different (now it
includes projects from step (3))
21. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
22. Open "Deployed" category
23. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
24. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above
25. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "South Asia" custom view
26. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different
27. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
28. Open "Deployed" category
29. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
30. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above

commit.message:
fix(api): Rate Limiter — Stale Bucket Eviction and Nested Lock Contention (#4592)

* Initial plan

* fix(api): rate limiter stale bucket eviction and nested lock contention

- Add TTL-based eviction in RateLimiter::allow(): buckets idle > 2×window
  with tokens >= capacity are removed to bound memory growth
- Replace std::mutex with std::shared_mutex in OperationRateLimiter so
  allow()/remaining()/getHeaders() take shared locks, setLimit()/clear()
  take exclusive locks — eliminates nested lock bottleneck under concurrency
- Compute steady_clock::now() before acquiring mutex_ in RateLimiter::allow()
  and remaining(); pass pre-computed time_point into Bucket::refill() and
  Bucket::consume() to keep clock syscalls outside the critical section
- Add <shared_mutex> include
- Update Bucket::refill() and Bucket::consume() signatures to accept
  a time_point parameter
- Add tests: StaleBucketEviction and ConcurrentReadsDontBlock

commit.message:
feat: pre-buffer next track audio at 80% for gapless transitions

Start filling a separate _next_prebuffer for the upcoming track when
playback reaches 80% of the current track's duration.  When the track
transition happens, get_audio_stream promotes the pre-filled buffer
so the first PCM chunk is available immediately — eliminating the
~590ms gap that caused buffer drain and audio artifacts on Bluetooth.

commit.message:
S3: delete directories in batches (#12910)

We were iterating over each file when deleting a directory. boto3 has
support for batch operations, it does one call for every 1K files, this

feat: Add file command autocompletion and job stage detection

Two improvements:

1. Enable autocompletion for file operation commands (rm, cp, mv, du)
   - Added these commands to the file-browsing completion list
   - Users can now type 'rm ' or 'cp ' and Tab to browse files
   - Improves usability for file operations

2. Detect and display job stage in 'run check' command
   - New detect_job_stage() function identifies which job was submitted
   - Analyzes SLURM output file for script names and keywords
   - Falls back to checking script modification times
   - Displays stage (PRE, MAIN, POST, etc.) in the status output
   - Users can now see at a glance which stage was last submitted

Examples:
  • rm file.txt<TAB>
  • cp /path/to/file .<TAB>

The improvements make file operations and job monitoring more intuitive.

the pipeline to prevent set -euo pipefail from propagating a non-zero

Move unparseable-output check (last_lint_exit!=0 && violation_count==0)
before the max-iterations-exhausted check. When MARKDOWNLINT_MAX_ITERATIONS=1
and the loop breaks on the last iteration due to unparseable output, the
exhaustion branch no longer fires first. Also tighten the exhaustion guard
to require final_violation_count > 0.

AGDT_PLAN_CONTEXT_BUDGET: check ^[0-9]+$ and 10#value > 0 to avoid

Improves code readability by making function names more descriptive.
The new MetaCoversPath name clearly indicates it checks if a meta rule
covers a specific path. It better conveys that it's a query function
rather than an action, and the applyToSubFolder parameter is more
explicit than applySub.

Also adds comprehensive test coverage:
- 10 tests for MetaCoversPath core logic
- 6 tests for CanWriteContent
UserPerms
- 7 tests for getReadme
- 5 tests for getHeader
- 6 tests for isEncrypt
- 9 tests for whetherHide

Total: 43 test scenarios covering all path matching and permission
inheritance logic. Tests verify both normal behavior and bug fixes
for Readme/Header information leakage and write permission bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(permission): implement fine-grained user permissions for read/write operations

Add per-user read and write permission controls at the meta level to enable
more granular access control beyond the existing permission flags.

Key changes:
- Add ReadUsers/WriteUsers fields to Meta model with sub-directory inheritance flags
- Implement CanRead and CanWrite permission check functions in server/common
- Filter file list results based on user read permissions
- Add permission checks across all file operations (FTP, HTTP handlers, WebDAV)
- Simplify error handling pattern for MetaNotFound errors throughout codebase

This allows administrators to restrict specific users from accessing or modifying
certain paths, providing finer control over file system permissions.

Note: Batch and recursive operations (FsMove, FsCopy, FsRemove, FsRecursiveMove,
FsBatchRename, FsRegexRename) currently check parent directory permissions only.
Individual item permission checks are not performed for performance reasons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(permission): add comprehensive tests for CanRead, CanWrite, and combined permission checks

Add TestCanRead, TestCanWrite, TestCanAccessWithReadPermissions, and
TestWritePermissionCombinations to validate the three-layer permission
system including nil user/meta, sub-path inheritance, user whitelists,
and root-level restrictions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(webdav): use safe type assertion for MetaPassKey to prevent panic

Bearer-token and OPTIONS auth paths do not set MetaPassKey in context,
causing a panic when handlers perform a forced type assertion on nil.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(permission): treat nil user as system context in CanRead/CanWrite

Previously, CanRead/CanWrite returned false for nil user, causing
filterReadableObjs to return an empty list when fs.List is called from
internal contexts without a user (e.g. context.Background()). A nil user
represents an internal/system call and should bypass per-user restrictions,
consistent with how whetherHide already handles nil user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(fsmanage): prevent path traversal in FsRemove

The previous check only skipped names that resolved to "/", but did not
prevent traversal to sibling directories (e.g. "../secret"), which could
bypass the CanWrite permission check that is only applied to req.Dir.

Replace with a post-join prefix check to ensure each resolved path stays
within reqPath.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(webdav): align MetaPassKey behavior with FTP auth logic

For guest users, the WebDAV password input serves as the meta folder
password (consistent with FTP anonymous/guest handling). For authenticated
users, MetaPassKey is set to empty string since their login password is
not the meta folder password.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(permission): require write auth for fs list refresh

* refactor(permission): use MetaCoversPath in CanRead/CanWrite for consistency

Replace inline `(Sub || meta.Path == path)` logic with MetaCoversPath,
consistent with CanWriteContentBypassUserPerms. Also fix a copy-paste
error in the CanWrite comment (read → write).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add parallel FFmpeg ABR HLS guide and hook scripts

Add documentation and automation for multi-instance adaptive bitrate
HLS encoding using parallel FFmpeg subscribers.

New files:
- scripts/on-publish-abr.sh  — Bash hook: 3 parallel transcoders
- scripts/on-publish-abr.ps1 — PowerShell equivalent

Key design decisions from review:
- Use -force_key_frames (time-based) instead of -g (frame-count)
  for fps-independent segment alignment
- Add -maxrate/-bufsize (VBV) for accurate BANDWIDTH reporting
- Clean stale segments on republish to prevent playlist corruption
- Remove append_list flag to avoid stale playlist accumulation
- Write master.m3u8 without BOM on Windows for parser compatibility
- Add #EXT-X-INDEPENDENT-SEGMENTS to master playlist

Documentation:
- hls-streaming.md: new Parallel FFmpeg ABR section with architecture
  diagram, alignment requirements, step-by-step, comparison table
- scripts/README.md: on-publish-abr entry and directory listing

* feat: use nbgv-python hatch plugin for Python versioning

Replace sed-based version stamping with nbgv-python hatch plugin that
natively produces PEP 440 versions (including pre-release normalization).

- Add nbgv-python>=2.0.0 to build-system requires
- Use dynamic version with hatch nbgv source plugin
- Bump Python to 3.12 in CI, CD, pyproject.toml, and ruff config
- Remove sed version hack from CD workflow

* feat: publish to GitHub Packages on main, public registries on release

CD now triggers on both main and release/** branches:
- release/** → NuGet.org, npmjs.com, PyPI (production)
- main → GitHub Packages (NuGet, npm), TestPyPI (Python)

npm GitHub Packages requires scoped names, so the publish step
temporarily sets the name to @rido-min/botas.

Additional secrets needed for non-release:
- TEST_PYPI_API_TOKEN (for TestPyPI)
- GITHUB_TOKEN is automatic (for NuGet/npm GitHub Packages)

* fix: use npmjs.com with --tag dev instead of GitHub Packages for npm

GitHub Packages npm requires scoped package names which would change
the package identity. Use npmjs.com with --tag dev for non-release
branches instead, keeping the same package name.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 3 complete — scheduler, approval gates, system tray

Backend:
- scheduler.ts: node-cron based pipeline scheduling. CRUD for schedules.
  Persists in .squad/schedules.json. Auto-starts enabled schedules on load.
  Cron validation, enable/disable toggle, event emission.
- system-tray.ts: Electron Tray with context menu, pending gate badge,
  next schedule tooltip. Click to show/hide. App stays in tray when
  schedules are active.
- Schedule IPC handlers (list, create, update, delete, toggle)
- Scheduler wired to pipeline engine — scheduled runs trigger notifications

Frontend:
- ScheduleView: list schedules with enabled toggle, cron expression,
  last run status. Create form with cron presets (hourly, daily, weekly)
  and custom cron input.
- ApprovalGateDialog: modal overlay with previous output display,
  approve/reject buttons. Wired to pipeline engine gate resolver.
- schedule-store: Zustand store for schedule CRUD
- Full CSS for schedules (cards, toggle switches, forms) and gates (dialog overlay)
- App.tsx wired to real ScheduleView

Tests:
- 7 new scheduler tests (cron validation, persistence, config handling)
- 62 total tests, all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
